target.title: Doctor: expose session artifact findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes legacy session transcript state as structured Doctor findings.
- Exposes stale cached session snapshot paths as structured Doctor findings.
- Treats session artifact findings as advisory `info` findings so default `doctor --lint` does not fail existing users with legacy artifacts.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for transcript rewrites and snapshot path rewrites.
- Keeps the existing legacy Doctor `run` paths authoritative for real `doctor` / `doctor --fix` mutations.
- Does not add or change config, plugin, or public SDK contract surface.

This is intentionally the next narrow session-artifacts slice before returning to the broader dry-run preview PR. It does not change transcript repair behavior, snapshot repair behavior, session store target resolution, or the public health-check contract.

## Stack

Rebased onto current `main` at `7668a72843d8437e193e0c3ed2467f88a8741512`. Current branch head is `c77bedfb7d` (`fix(doctor): make session artifact findings advisory`).

## Validation

Current head `c77bedfb7d`:

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 9 tests passed
- Source CLI proof with a temp legacy transcript and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` override:
  - `corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-transcripts` - exit 0, `ok: true`, no visible findings at the default `warning` threshold
  - `corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json --severity-min info --only core/doctor/session-transcripts` - exit 1, surfaced one `info` `core/doctor/session-transcripts` finding
- Direct source adapter check via `node --import tsx` confirmed both transcript and snapshot artifact adapters emit `info` severity
- `node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD src\commands\doctor-session-transcripts.ts src\commands\doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts src\commands\doctor-session-snapshots.ts src\commands\doctor-session-snapshots.test.ts`
- `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check src\commands\doctor-session-transcripts.ts src\commands\doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts src\commands\doctor-session-snapshots.ts src\commands\doctor-session-snapshots.test.ts`
- `node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts\tsgo-cache\core.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test\tsconfig\tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts\tsgo-cache\test-src.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

Previous head `88b539ebbc` before the advisory-severity follow-up also passed the broader focused shard: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 76 tests passed across 2 Vitest shards.

## Notes

A local Windows rerun of the full snapshot repair test file still hits existing path-escaping assertions in repair tests that compare unescaped Windows paths against JSON-escaped file contents. The advisory-severity change is covered by the snapshot adapter assertion update, the direct adapter check, typecheck, lint, format, and the real source CLI lint threshold proof above.
target.author: giodl73-repo
